summary: ethers-common executes remote shell command during npm install
details: The package.json lifecycle script at line 7 uses require('child_process') and execSync('curl...') to fetch and execute remote content during npm install. The package name mimics the widely-used 'ethers' library, and its install hook runs an unpinned curl-to-shell pattern rather than performing any legitimate package setup. This grants the publisher arbitrary code execution on any machine that installs the package, with no integrity verification and no documented purpose consistent with a library named 'ethers-common'. The combination of a typosquat-style name against a top-tier Ethereum library plus install-time remote execution is an unambiguous supply-chain attack shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
